name: Strawberry Cheesecake (Rodier)
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush fresh strawberries or thaw frozen unsweetened strawberries.
2. For 1/3 cup evaporated milk, place a scant 2 tablespoons powdered skim milk in a measuring cup and fill with liquid milk up to the 1/3 mark.
3. Preheat oven to 400°F. Melt margarine in a 9-inch pan. Add crumbs and mix. Press mixture over the bottom of the pan. Bake for 5 to 7 minutes, then cool.
4. Sieve cottage cheese or puree in a blender until smooth. Add milk and stir until smooth. Chill.
5. In a saucepan, mix 1 1/2 teaspoons gelatin and 1 tablespoon sugar. Add orange juice and rind. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly until gelatin is dissolved. Remove from heat and let stand at room temperature.
6. In a medium bowl, beat egg whites with salt until stiff. Fold in gelatin and cottage cheese mixture. Pour over crumbs.
7. Refrigerate until set before adding glaze.
8. For the glaze, mix remaining gelatin (1/2 tablespoon) with water and 1 tablespoon sugar. Heat until gelatin is dissolved. Stir in strawberries. Pour mixture over cheesecake and spread with a spatula.
9. Refrigerate until firm.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
